NKJV.1: A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD, And let my cry come to You.
NKJV.2: Do not hide Your face from me in the day of my trouble; Incline Your ear to me; In the day that I call, answer me speedily.
NKJV.3: For my days are consumed like smoke, And my bones are burned like a hearth.
NKJV.4: My heart is stricken and withered like grass, So that I forget to eat my bread.
NKJV.5: Because of the sound of my groaning My bones cling to my skin.
NKJV.6: I am like a pelican of the wilderness; I am like an owl of the desert.
NKJV.7: I lie awake, And am like a sparrow alone on the housetop.
NKJV.8: My enemies reproach me all day long; Those who deride me swear an oath against me.
NKJV.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread, And mingled my drink with weeping,
NKJV.10: Because of Your indignation and Your wrath; For You have lifted me up and cast me away.
NKJV.11: My days are like a shadow that lengthens, And I wither away like grass.
NKJV.12: But You, O LORD, shall endure forever, And the remembrance of Your name to all generations.
NKJV.13: You will arise and have mercy on Zion; For the time to favor her, Yes, the set time, has come.
NKJV.14: For Your servants take pleasure in her stones, And show favor to her dust.
NKJV.15: So the nations shall fear the name of the LORD, And all the kings of the earth Your glory.
NKJV.16: For the LORD shall build up Zion;  He shall appear in His glory.
NKJV.17: He shall regard the prayer of the destitute, And shall not despise their prayer.
NKJV.18: This will be written for the generation to come, That a people yet to be created may praise the LORD.
NKJV.19: For He looked down from the height of His sanctuary; From heaven the LORD viewed the earth,
NKJV.20: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, To release those appointed to death,
NKJV.21: To declare the name of the LORD in Zion, And His praise in Jerusalem,
NKJV.22: When the peoples are gathered together, And the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
NKJV.23: He weakened my strength in the way; He shortened my days.
NKJV.24: I said, “O my God, Do not take me away in the midst of my days; Your years are throughout all generations.
NKJV.25: Of old You laid the foundation of the earth, And the heavens are the work of Your hands.
NKJV.26: They will perish, but You will endure; Yes, they will all grow old like a garment; Like a cloak You will change them, And they will be changed.
NKJV.27: But You are the same, And Your years will have no end.
NKJV.28: The children of Your servants will continue, And their descendants will be established before You.”

ESV.1: Hear my prayer, O LORD; let my cry come to you!
ESV.2: Do not hide your face from me in the day of my distress! Incline your ear to me; answer me speedily in the day when I call!
ESV.3: For my days pass away like smoke, and my bones burn like a furnace.
ESV.4: My heart is struck down like grass and has withered; I forget to eat my bread.
ESV.5: Because of my loud groaning my bones cling to my flesh.
ESV.6: I am like a desert owl of the wilderness, like an owl  of the waste places;
ESV.7: I lie awake; I am like a lonely sparrow on the housetop.
ESV.8: All the day my enemies taunt me; those who deride me use my name for a curse.
ESV.9: For I eat ashes like bread and mingle tears with my drink,
ESV.10: because of your indignation and anger; for you have taken me up and thrown me down.
ESV.11: My days are like an evening shadow; I wither away like grass.
ESV.12: But you, O LORD, are enthroned forever; you are remembered throughout all generations.
ESV.13: You will arise and have pity on Zion; it is the time to favor her; the appointed time has come.
ESV.14: For your servants hold her stones dear and have pity on her dust.
ESV.15: Nations will fear the name of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth will fear your glory.
ESV.16: For the LORD builds up Zion; he appears in his glory;
ESV.17: he regards the prayer of the destitute and does not despise their prayer.
ESV.18: Let this be recorded for a generation to come, so that a people yet to be created may praise the LORD:
ESV.19: that he looked down from his holy height; from heaven the LORD looked at the earth,
ESV.20: to hear the groans of the prisoners, to set free those who were doomed to die,
ESV.21: that they may declare in Zion the name of the LORD, and in Jerusalem his praise,
ESV.22: when peoples gather together, and kingdoms, to worship the LORD.
ESV.23: He has broken my strength in midcourse; he has shortened my days.
ESV.24: “O my God,” I say, “take me not away in the midst of my days — you whose years endure throughout all generations!”
ESV.25: Of old you laid the foundation of the earth, and the heavens are the work of your hands.
ESV.26: They will perish, but you will remain; they will all wear out like a garment. You will change them like a robe, and they will pass away,
ESV.27: but you are the same, and your years have no end.
ESV.28: The children of your servants shall dwell secure; their offspring shall be established before you.

NASB.1: A Prayer of the Afflicted when he is faint and pours out his complaint before the LORD. Hear my prayer, O LORD! And let my cry for help come to You.
NASB.2: Do not hide Your face from me in the day of my distress; Incline Your ear to me; In the day when I call answer me quickly.
NASB.3: For my days have been consumed in smoke, And my bones have been scorched like a hearth.
NASB.4: My heart has been smitten like grass and has withered away, Indeed, I forget to eat my bread.
NASB.5: Because of the loudness of my groaning My bones cling to my flesh.
NASB.6: I resemble a pelican of the wilderness; I have become like an owl of the waste places.
NASB.7: I lie awake, I have become like a lonely bird on a housetop.
NASB.8: My enemies have reproached me all day long; Those who deride me have used my [name] as a curse.
NASB.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread And mingled my drink with weeping
NASB.10: Because of Your indignation and Your wrath, For You have lifted me up and cast me away.
NASB.11: My days are like a lengthened shadow, And I wither away like grass.
NASB.12: But You, O LORD, abide forever, And Your name to all generations.
NASB.13: You will arise [and] have compassion on Zion; For it is time to be gracious to her, For the appointed time has come.
NASB.14: Surely Your servants find pleasure in her stones And feel pity for her dust.
NASB.15: So the nations will fear the name of the LORD And all the kings of the earth Your glory.
NASB.16: For the LORD has built up Zion; He has appeared in His glory.
NASB.17: He has regarded the prayer of the destitute And has not despised their prayer.
NASB.18: This will be written for the generation to come, That a people yet to be created may praise the LORD.
NASB.19: For He looked down from His holy height; From heaven the LORD gazed upon the earth,
NASB.20: To hear the groaning of the prisoner, To set free those who were doomed to death,
NASB.21: That [men] may tell of the name of the LORD in Zion And His praise in Jerusalem,
NASB.22: When the peoples are gathered together, And the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
NASB.23: He has weakened my strength in the way; He has shortened my days.
NASB.24: I say, 'O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days, Your years are throughout all generations.
NASB.25: 'Of old You founded the earth, And the heavens are the work of Your hands.
NASB.26: 'Even they will perish, but You endure; And all of them will wear out like a garment; Like clothing You will change them and they will be changed.
NASB.27: 'But You are the same, And Your years will not come to an end.
NASB.28: 'The children of Your servants will continue, And their descendants will be established before You.'

NLT.1:
LORD, hear my prayer!
Listen to my plea!
NLT.2:
Don’t turn away from me
in my time of distress.
Bend down to listen,
and answer me quickly when I call to you.
NLT.3:
For my days disappear like smoke,
and my bones burn like red-hot coals.
NLT.4:
My heart is sick, withered like grass,
and I have lost my appetite.
NLT.5:
Because of my groaning,
I am reduced to skin and bones.
NLT.6:
I am like an owl in the desert,
like a little owl in a far-off wilderness.
NLT.7:
I lie awake,
lonely as a solitary bird on the roof.
NLT.8:
My enemies taunt me day after day.
They mock and curse me.
NLT.9:
I eat ashes for food.
My tears run down into my drink
NLT.10:
because of your anger and wrath.
For you have picked me up and thrown me out.
NLT.11:
My life passes as swiftly as the evening shadows.
I am withering away like grass.
NLT.12:
But you, O LORD, will sit on your throne forever.
Your fame will endure to every generation.
NLT.13:
You will arise and have mercy on Jerusalem —
and now is the time to pity her,
now is the time you promised to help.
NLT.14:
For your people love every stone in her walls
and cherish even the dust in her streets.
NLT.15:
Then the nations will tremble before the LORD.
The kings of the earth will tremble before his glory.
NLT.16:
For the LORD will rebuild Jerusalem.
He will appear in his glory.
NLT.17:
He will listen to the prayers of the destitute.
He will not reject their pleas.
NLT.18:
Let this be recorded for future generations,
so that a people not yet born will praise the LORD.
NLT.19:
Tell them the LORD looked down
from his heavenly sanctuary.
He looked down to earth from heaven
NLT.20:
to hear the groans of the prisoners,
to release those condemned to die.
NLT.21:
And so the LORD’s fame will be celebrated in Zion,
his praises in Jerusalem,
NLT.22:
when multitudes gather together
and kingdoms come to worship the LORD.
NLT.23:
He broke my strength in midlife,
cutting short my days.
NLT.24:
But I cried to him, “O my God, who lives forever,
don’t take my life while I am so young!
NLT.25:
Long ago you laid the foundation of the earth
and made the heavens with your hands.
NLT.26:
They will perish, but you remain forever;
they will wear out like old clothing.
You will change them like a garment
and discard them.
NLT.27:
But you are always the same;
you will live forever.
NLT.28:
The children of your people
will live in security.
Their children’s children
will thrive in your presence.”

WEB.1: A Prayer of the afflicted, when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint before Yahweh. Hear my prayer, Yahweh! Let my cry come to you.
WEB.2: Don’t hide your face from me in the day of my distress. Turn your ear to me. Answer me quickly in the day when I call.
WEB.3: For my days consume away like smoke. My bones are burned as a torch.
WEB.4: My heart is blighted like grass, and withered, for I forget to eat my bread.
WEB.5: By reason of the voice of my groaning, my bones stick to my skin.
WEB.6: I am like a pelican of the wilderness. I have become as an owl of the waste places.
WEB.7: I watch, and have become like a sparrow that is alone on the housetop.
WEB.8: My enemies reproach me all day. Those who are mad at me use my name as a curse.
WEB.9: For I have eaten ashes like bread, and mixed my drink with tears,
WEB.10: Because of your indignation and your wrath, for you have taken me up, and thrown me away.
WEB.11: My days are like a long shadow. I have withered like grass.
WEB.12: But you, Yahweh, will remain forever; your renown endures to all generations.
WEB.13: You will arise and have mercy on Zion; for it is time to have pity on her. Yes, the set time has come.
WEB.14: For your servants take pleasure in her stones, and have pity on her dust.
WEB.15: So the nations will fear Yahweh’s name; all the kings of the earth your glory.
WEB.16: For Yahweh has built up Zion. He has appeared in his glory.
WEB.17: He has responded to the prayer of the destitute, and has not despised their prayer.
WEB.18: This will be written for the generation to come. A people which will be created will praise Yah.
WEB.19: For he has looked down from the height of his sanctuary. From heaven, Yahweh saw the earth;
WEB.20: to hear the groans of the prisoner; to free those who are condemned to death;
WEB.21: that men may declare Yahweh’s name in Zion, and his praise in Jerusalem;
WEB.22: when the peoples are gathered together, the kingdoms, to serve Yahweh.
WEB.23: He weakened my strength along the course. He shortened my days.
WEB.24: I said, “My God, don’t take me away in the middle of my days. Your years are throughout all generations.
WEB.25: Of old, you laid the foundation of the earth. The heavens are the work of your hands.
WEB.26: They will perish, but you will endure. Yes, all of them will wear out like a garment. You will change them like a cloak, and they will be changed.
WEB.27: But you are the same. Your years will have no end.
WEB.28: The children of your servants will continue. Their offspring  will be established before you.”

CSB17.1:
A prayer of a suffering person who is weak and pours out his lament before the LORD. ⓕ [1] LORD, hear my prayer;
let my cry for help come before you. ⓖ
CSB17.2:
Do not hide your face from me in my day of trouble.
Listen closely to me;
answer me quickly when I call. ⓗ
CSB17.3:
For my days vanish like smoke,
and my bones burn like a furnace. ⓘ
CSB17.4:
My heart is suffering, withered like grass; ⓙ
I even forget to eat my food. ⓚ
CSB17.5:
Because of the sound of my groaning,
my flesh sticks to my bones. ⓛ
CSB17.6:
I am like an eagle owl,
like a little owl among the ruins. ⓜ
CSB17.7:
I stay awake; ⓝ
I am like a solitary bird on a roof.
CSB17.8:
My enemies taunt me all day long;
they ridicule and use my name as a curse. ⓐ
CSB17.9:
I eat ashes like bread
and mingle my drinks with tears ⓑ
CSB17.10:
because of your indignation and wrath;
for you have picked me up and thrown me aside. ⓒ
CSB17.11:
My days are like a lengthening shadow,
and I wither away like grass. ⓓ
CSB17.12:
But you, LORD, are enthroned forever; ⓔ
your fame endures to all generations. ⓕ
CSB17.13:
You will rise up and have compassion on Zion,
for it is time to show favor to her —
the appointed time has come. ⓖ
CSB17.14:
For your servants take delight in its stones
and favor its dust. ⓗ
CSB17.15:
Then the nations will fear the name of the LORD,
and all the kings of the earth your glory, ⓘ
CSB17.16:
for the LORD will rebuild Zion;
he will appear in his glory. ⓙ
CSB17.17:
He will pay attention to the prayer of the destitute
and will not despise their prayer. ⓚ
CSB17.18:
This will be written for a later generation,
and a people who have not yet been created will praise the LORD: ⓛ
CSB17.19:
He looked down from his holy heights —
the LORD gazed out from heaven to earth ⓜ  —
CSB17.20:
to hear a prisoner’s groaning,
to set free those condemned to die, [1] ⓝ
CSB17.21:
so that they might declare
the name of the LORD in Zion
and his praise in Jerusalem, ⓐ
CSB17.22:
when peoples and kingdoms are assembled
to serve the LORD. ⓑ
CSB17.23:
He has broken my [2] strength in midcourse;
he has shortened my days. ⓒ
CSB17.24:
I say: “My God, do not take me
in the middle of my life! [3]
Your years continue through all generations. ⓓ
CSB17.25:
Long ago you established the earth,
and the heavens are the work of your hands. ⓔ
CSB17.26:
They will perish, but you will endure;
all of them will wear out like clothing.
You will change them like a garment,
and they will pass away. ⓕ
CSB17.27:
But you are the same,
and your years will never end. ⓖ
CSB17.28:
Your servants’ children will dwell securely,
and their offspring will be established before you.” ⓗ

AMP.1:
A Prayer of the afflicted; when he is overwhelmed and pours out his complaint to God. Hear my prayer, O LORD,
And let my cry for help come to You!
AMP.2:
Do not hide Your face from me in the day of my distress!
Incline Your ear to me;
In the day when I call, answer me quickly.
AMP.3:
For my days have vanished in smoke,
And my bones have been scorched like a hearth.
AMP.4:
My heart has been struck like grass and withered,
Indeed, [absorbed by my heartache] I forget to eat my food.
AMP.5:
Because of the sound of my groaning [in suffering and trouble]
My bones cling to my flesh.
AMP.6:
I am like a [mournful] vulture of the wilderness;
I am like a [desolate] owl of the wasteland.
AMP.7:
I am sleepless and lie awake [mourning],
I have become like a lonely bird on a housetop.
AMP.8:
My enemies taunt me all day long;
Those who ridicule me use my name as a curse.
AMP.9:
For I have eaten ashes like bread,
And have mingled my drink with tears
AMP.10:
Because of Your indignation and Your wrath,
For You have lifted me up and thrown me away.
AMP.11:
My days are like an evening shadow that lengthens and vanishes [with the sun];
And as for me, I wither away like grass.
AMP.12:
But You, O LORD, are enthroned forever [ruling eternally as sovereign];
And [the fame and glory of] Your name [endures] to all generations.
AMP.13:
You will arise and have compassion on Zion,
For it is time to be gracious and show favor to her;
Yes, the appointed time [the moment designated] has come.
AMP.14:
For Your servants find [melancholy] pleasure in the stones [of her ruins]
And feel pity for her dust.
AMP.15:
So the nations will fear the name of the LORD,
And all the kings of the earth [will recognize] Your glory.
AMP.16:
For the LORD has built up Zion;
He has appeared in His glory and brilliance;
AMP.17:
He has regarded the prayer of the destitute,
And has not despised their prayer.
AMP.18:
Let this be recorded for the generation to come,
That a people yet to be created will praise the LORD.
AMP.19:
For He looked down from His holy height [of His sanctuary],
From heaven the LORD gazed on the earth,
AMP.20:
To hear the sighing of the prisoner,
To set free those who were doomed to death,
AMP.21:
So that people may declare the name of the LORD in Zion
And His praise in Jerusalem,
AMP.22:
When the peoples are gathered together,
And the kingdoms, to serve the LORD.
AMP.23:
He has exhausted my strength [humbling me with sorrow] in the way;
He has shortened my days.
AMP.24:
I said, "O my God, do not take me away in the midst of my days;
Your years are [eternal] throughout all generations.
AMP.25:
"At the beginning You founded the earth;
The heavens are the work of Your hands.
AMP.26:
"Even they will perish, but You endure;
Yes, all of them will wear out like a garment.
Like clothing You will change them and they shall be changed.
AMP.27:
"But You remain the same,
And Your years will never end.
AMP.28:
"The children of Your servants will continue,
And their descendants will be established before You."

MSG.1:
A prayer of one whose life is falling to pieces, and who lets God know just how bad it is.
God, listen! Listen to my prayer,
listen to the pain in my cries.
MSG.2:
Don't turn your back on me
just when I need you so desperately.
Pay attention! This is a cry for help!
And hurry—this can't wait!
MSG.3:
I'm wasting away to nothing,
I'm burning up with fever.
MSG.4:
I'm a ghost of my former self,
half-consumed already by terminal illness.
MSG.5:
My jaws ache from gritting my teeth;
I'm nothing but skin and bones.
MSG.6:
I'm like a buzzard in the desert,
a crow perched on the rubble.
MSG.7:
Insomniac, I twitter away,
mournful as a sparrow in the gutter.
MSG.8:
All day long my enemies taunt me,
while others just curse.
MSG.9:
They bring in meals—casseroles of ashes!
I draw drink from a barrel of my tears.
MSG.10:
And all because of your furious anger;
you swept me up and threw me out.
MSG.11:
There's nothing left of me—
a withered weed, swept clean from the path.
MSG.12:
Yet you, God, are sovereign still,
always and ever sovereign.
MSG.13:
You'll get up from your throne and help Zion—
it's time for compassionate help.
MSG.14:
Oh, how your servants love this city's rubble
and weep with compassion over its dust!
MSG.15:
The godless nations will sit up and take notice
—see your glory, worship your name—
MSG.16:
When God rebuilds Zion,
when he shows up in all his glory,
MSG.17:
When he attends to the prayer of the wretched.
He won't dismiss their prayer.
MSG.18:
Write this down for the next generation
so people not yet born will praise God:
MSG.19:
"God looked out from his high holy place;
from heaven he surveyed the earth.
MSG.20:
He listened to the groans of the doomed,
he opened the doors of their death cells."
MSG.21:
Write it so the story can be told in Zion,
so God's praise will be sung in Jerusalem's streets
MSG.22:
And wherever people gather together
along with their rulers to worship him.
MSG.23:
God sovereignly brought me to my knees,
he cut me down in my prime.
MSG.24:
"Oh, don't," I prayed, "please don't let me die.
You have more years than you know what to do with!
MSG.25:
You laid earth's foundations a long time ago,
and handcrafted the very heavens;
MSG.26:
You'll still be around when they're long gone,
threadbare and discarded like an old suit of clothes.
You'll throw them away like a worn-out coat,
MSG.27: but year after year you're as good as new.
MSG.28:
Your servants' children will have a good place to live
and their children will be at home with you."

NIV.1:
Psalm 102
A prayer of an afflicted man. When he is faint and pours out his lament before the Lord.
Hear my prayer, O Lord;
let my cry for help come to you.
NIV.2:
Do not hide your face from me
when I am in distress.
Turn your ear to me;
when I call, answer me quickly.
NIV.3:
For my days vanish like smoke;
my bones burn like glowing embers.
NIV.4:
My heart is blighted and withered like grass;
I forget to eat my food.
NIV.5:
Because of my loud groaning
I am reduced to skin and bones.
NIV.6:
I am like a desert owl,
like an owl among the ruins.
NIV.7:
I lie awake; I have become
like a bird alone on a roof.
NIV.8:
All day long my enemies taunt me;
those who rail against me use my name as a curse.
NIV.9:
For I eat ashes as my food
and mingle my drink with tears
NIV.10:
because of your great wrath,
for you have taken me up and thrown me aside.
NIV.11:
My days are like the evening shadow;
I wither away like grass.
NIV.12:
But you, O Lord, sit enthroned forever;
your renown endures through all generations.
NIV.13:
You will arise and have compassion on Zion,
for it is time to show favor to her;
the appointed time has come.
NIV.14:
For her stones are dear to your servants;
her very dust moves them to pity.
NIV.15:
The nations will fear the name of the Lord,
all the kings of the earth will revere your glory.
NIV.16:
For the Lord will rebuild Zion
and appear in his glory.
NIV.17:
He will respond to the prayer of the destitute;
he will not despise their plea.
NIV.18:
Let this be written for a future generation,
that a people not yet created may praise the Lord:
NIV.19:
“The Lord looked down from his sanctuary on high,
from heaven he viewed the earth,
NIV.20:
to hear the groans of the prisoners
and release those condemned to death.”
NIV.21:
So the name of the Lord will be declared in Zion
and his praise in Jerusalem
NIV.22:
when the peoples and the kingdoms
assemble to worship the Lord.
NIV.23:
In the course of my life he broke my strength;
he cut short my days.
NIV.24:
So I said:
“Do not take me away, O my God, in the midst of my days;
your years go on through all generations.
NIV.25:
In the beginning you laid the foundations of the earth,
and the heavens are the work of your hands.
NIV.26:
They will perish, but you remain;
they will all wear out like a garment.
Like clothing you will change them
and they will be discarded.
NIV.27:
But you remain the same,
and your years will never end.
NIV.28:
The children of your servants will live in your presence;
their descendants will be established before you.”

NRSVCE.1:
Hear my prayer, O LORD;
let my cry come to you.
NRSVCE.2:
Do not hide your face from me
in the day of my distress.
Incline your ear to me;
answer me speedily in the day when I call.
NRSVCE.3:
For my days pass away like smoke,
and my bones burn like a furnace.
NRSVCE.4:
My heart is stricken and withered like grass;
I am too wasted to eat my bread.
NRSVCE.5:
Because of my loud groaning
my bones cling to my skin.
NRSVCE.6:
I am like an owl of the wilderness,
like a little owl of the waste places.
NRSVCE.7:
I lie awake;
I am like a lonely bird on the housetop.
NRSVCE.8:
All day long my enemies taunt me;
those who deride me use my name for a curse.
NRSVCE.9:
For I eat ashes like bread,
and mingle tears with my drink,
NRSVCE.10:
because of your indignation and anger;
for you have lifted me up and thrown me aside.
NRSVCE.11:
My days are like an evening shadow;
I wither away like grass.
NRSVCE.12:
But you, O LORD, are enthroned forever;
your name endures to all generations.
NRSVCE.13:
You will rise up and have compassion on Zion,
for it is time to favor it;
the appointed time has come.
NRSVCE.14:
For your servants hold its stones dear,
and have pity on its dust.
NRSVCE.15:
The nations will fear the name of the LORD,
and all the kings of the earth your glory.
NRSVCE.16:
For the LORD will build up Zion;
he will appear in his glory.
NRSVCE.17:
He will regard the prayer of the destitute,
and will not despise their prayer.
NRSVCE.18:
Let this be recorded for a generation to come,
so that a people yet unborn may praise the LORD:
NRSVCE.19:
that he looked down from his holy height,
from heaven the LORD looked at the earth,
NRSVCE.20:
to hear the groans of the prisoners,
to set free those who were doomed to die;
NRSVCE.21:
so that the name of the LORD may be declared in Zion,
and his praise in Jerusalem,
NRSVCE.22:
when peoples gather together,
and kingdoms, to worship the LORD.
NRSVCE.23:
He has broken my strength in midcourse;
he has shortened my days.
NRSVCE.24:
“O my God,” I say, “do not take me away
at the midpoint of my life,
you whose years endure
throughout all generations.”
NRSVCE.25:
Long ago you laid the foundation of the earth,
and the heavens are the work of your hands.
NRSVCE.26:
They will perish, but you endure;
they will all wear out like a garment.
You change them like clothing, and they pass away;
NRSVCE.27: but you are the same, and your years have no end.
NRSVCE.28:
The children of your servants shall live secure;
their offspring shall be established in your presence.

NET.1: O LORD, hear my prayer! Pay attention to my cry for help!
NET.2: Do not ignore me in my time of trouble! Listen to me! When I call out to you, quickly answer me!
NET.3: For my days go up in smoke, and my bones are charred like a fireplace.
NET.4: My heart is parched and withered like grass, for I am unable to eat food.
NET.5: Because of the anxiety that makes me groan, my bones protrude from my skin.
NET.6: I am like an owl in the wilderness; I am like a screech owl among the ruins.
NET.7: I stay awake; I am like a solitary bird on a roof.
NET.8: All day long my enemies taunt me; those who mock me use my name in their curses.
NET.9: For I eat ashes as if they were bread, and mix my drink with my tears,
NET.10: because of your anger and raging fury. Indeed, you pick me up and throw me away.
NET.11: My days are coming to an end, and I am withered like grass.
NET.12: But you, O LORD, rule forever, and your reputation endures.
NET.13: You will rise up and have compassion on Zion. For it is time to have mercy on her, for the appointed time has come.
NET.14: Indeed, your servants take delight in her stones, and feel compassion for the dust of her ruins.
NET.15: The nations will respect the reputation of the LORD, and all the kings of the earth will respect his splendor,
NET.16: when the LORD rebuilds Zion, and reveals his splendor,
NET.17: when he responds to the prayer of the destitute, and does not reject their request.
NET.18: The account of his intervention will be recorded for future generations; people yet to be born will praise the LORD.
NET.19: For he will look down from his sanctuary above; from heaven the LORD will look toward earth,
NET.20: in order to hear the painful cries of the prisoners, and to set free those condemned to die,
NET.21: so they may proclaim the name of the LORD in Zion, and praise him in Jerusalem,
NET.22: when the nations gather together, and the kingdoms pay tribute to the LORD.
NET.23: He has taken away my strength in the middle of life; he has cut short my days.
NET.24: I say, “O my God, please do not take me away in the middle of my life! You endure through all generations.
NET.25: In earlier times you established the earth; the skies are your handiwork.
NET.26: They will perish, but you will endure. They will wear out like a garment; like clothes you will remove them and they will disappear.
NET.27: But you remain; your years do not come to an end.
NET.28: The children of your servants will settle down here, and their descendants will live securely in your presence.”
